title: Sign the Mega-Pipeline?
briefing: A continent-spanning pipeline is one signature away from breaking ground. Backers promise cheaper fuel and tens of thousands of construction jobs; tribes, ranchers, and climate groups have chained themselves to the proposed route. Energy for the grid, or a fight you'll be litigating for a decade?

choices.label: Approve the pipeline
choices.result: Steel starts shipping within weeks and energy futures dip. Protest camps swell along the route and the courts fill with injunctions.

news.headline: Pipeline greenlit: jobs and energy independence flow
President paves a carbon superhighway through sacred land
Administration approves long-disputed pipeline
POTUS SIGNS THE PIPE — protesters storm the prairie! 🛢️🔥

social.text: two years of steady work just landed in my lap. about time.
they ran it straight through our water. we're not moving.

choices.advisor: The jobs are real and immediate. So are the lawsuits — budget for a long, ugly courtroom decade.

choices.label: Demand a costly reroute
choices.detail: Force it around protected land and water.
choices.result: You order the route bent around the sensitive corridor. Both sides grumble — too expensive for industry, too compromised for purists — but the camps quietly fold.

news.headline: President forces pipeline reroute to cool tensions
Red tape adds billions and years to vital project
A partial win: the water is spared, the pipe is not
POTUS BENDS THE PIPE — nobody's happy, everybody's loud! 😤

social.text: a compromise where both sides are annoyed? that's basically governing.

choices.advisor: Splitting the baby costs money and thrills nobody, but it empties the protest camps. Sometimes quiet is the win.

choices.label: Kill the project
choices.result: You revoke the permit for good. Climate groups throw a party; energy markets twitch and a region's promised paychecks vanish overnight.

news.headline: President kills the pipeline — a line in the sand for the climate
Thousands of jobs torched to please activists
Administration cancels disputed pipeline permit
POTUS PULLS THE PLUG on the pipe — boomtowns BUST! 💸

social.text: my grandkids will drink that water. worth every angry headline.

choices.advisor: A legacy-defining no. Just know the laid-off welders vote too, and they'll remember.

title: Cap the Smokestacks?
briefing: Your team has drafted the strictest carbon caps in the nation's history. Hit the targets and you lead the world on climate; miss the politics and you hand industry a rallying cry about shuttered plants and higher bills. How hard do you squeeze?

choices.label: Impose the strict cap
choices.result: Emissions targets become law with no soft landing. Green investors cheer, heavy industry warns of layoffs, and your poll numbers in factory states sag.

news.headline: Boldest climate cap in history clears the desk
War on industry: mandate threatens the factory floor
President signs sweeping emissions limits
POTUS SLAMS the smokestacks — bills set to SOAR?! 🌫️

social.text: first time my city's asthma map might actually shrink. i'm crying.

choices.advisor: History will love this; the rust belt won't. The economy will take a real bruise before the upside lands.

choices.label: Phase it in slowly
choices.detail: Long runway, industry credits, softer targets.
choices.result: You set gentle, escalating targets with credits for early movers. The reaction is a collective shrug — exactly the goal.

news.headline: President opts for gradual emissions phase-in
Industry breathes easier under softened timeline
Climate cap watered down before the ink dries
POTUS goes EASY on the smoke — greens grumble 😒

social.text: a timeline i can actually plan around. i'll take it.

choices.advisor: Pragmatic and quiet. You buy peace now and trade away a chunk of the legacy headline.

choices.label: Scrap the caps entirely
choices.result: You shelve the rules and lean into 'energy abundance.' Markets rally and industry sends thank-you notes; climate scientists send open letters.

news.headline: Common sense wins: job-killing caps shelved
President abandons the climate fight before it starts
Administration drops proposed emissions caps
POTUS KILLS the carbon rules — planet left on read! 🌍❌

social.text: you can't run a country on good intentions. growth first.

choices.advisor: Wall Street loves it today. The history books are already drafting a less flattering chapter.

title: The Megafire Spreads
briefing: A record drought has turned the western forests into a tinderbox, and three fires just merged into one. Towns are evacuating, the smoke has reached the coasts, and your phone won't stop. Throw everything at the flames now, or hold resources and confront why this keeps happening?

choices.label: Surge every resource now
choices.result: You federalize the response and the sky fills with tankers. The line holds outside the biggest town and the cameras catch you hugging firefighters.

news.headline: President mounts massive federal firefight
POTUS GOES ALL IN on the megafire — TOWN SAVED at dawn! 🔥🚁
Heroic response, but the drought question goes unanswered
Disaster bill balloons as Washington opens the spigot

social.text: watched the tankers turn the fire back from our ridge. thank you isn't enough.

choices.advisor: Showing up wins the week. Just don't let 'we fought hard' become an excuse to skip 'why again?'

choices.label: Fund prevention & resilience
choices.detail: Controlled burns, forest thinning, building codes.
choices.result: You pair the response with a major resilience package — controlled burns, fire-wise codes, drought infrastructure. Experts applaud; some voters wanted to see you on the fire line instead.

news.headline: President treats the cause, not just the smoke
Administration unveils wildfire resilience plan
More mandates and burn rules dumped on rural towns
POTUS lights MORE fires (on purpose?!) to stop the big one 🤔🔥

social.text: controlled burns work. we've begged for this funding for 20 years.

choices.advisor: Smart, unglamorous, and it pays off three fire seasons from now — long after the cameras leave.

choices.label: Leave it to the states
choices.result: You frame fire as a state matter and keep federal cash on a short leash. Governors fume, the fire jumps the line, and a town you could've saved makes the evening news.

news.headline: President holds the line on federal spending
Towns burn as Washington shrugs
President defers wildfire response to states
POTUS SITS OUT the megafire — governors RAGE! 😡🔥

social.text: we called for help and got a press release. our street is gone.

choices.advisor: Fiscally tidy, politically radioactive. A disaster is the one place 'not my job' never plays.

title: Mandate the Electric Future?
briefing: Your draft rule would phase out new gas-engine cars within a decade. Automakers are split, autoworkers are nervous, and the charging network barely exists outside the cities. Floor it toward the electric future, or pump the brakes before the public revolts?

choices.label: Set the hard deadline
choices.result: You lock in the phase-out date. EV makers and battery towns boom; truck-country voters hear 'they're taking your engine' and the backlash writes itself.

news.headline: President sets the date the gas engine ends
Government picks your next car — and it isn't ready
Administration mandates EV transition timeline
POTUS BANS the gas pedal (eventually) — pickup nation REVOLTS! ⚡🚗

social.text: our gigafactory just announced a third shift. the future has a zip code now.

choices.advisor: The right long arc, the wrong charging map. You'll be defending range anxiety at every town hall.

choices.label: Incentivize, don't mandate
choices.result: You pour money into rebates and chargers and let buyers choose. Adoption climbs steadily and almost nobody feels forced — but the gas era doesn't end on schedule.

news.headline: President bets on carrots over mandates for EVs
Consumer choice survives the green push
Voluntary plan may be too slow to matter
POTUS BRIBES you to go electric — free charger near you?! 🔌💰

social.text: took the rebate, love the car, nobody twisted my arm. this is how you do it.

choices.advisor: Popular and durable. It's slower than the science wants, but a rule nobody resents actually sticks.

choices.label: Shelve the mandate
choices.result: You side with the engine and kill the timeline. Dealers and gearheads celebrate; battery startups and climate hawks feel the rug pulled.

news.headline: President saves the American gas engine
EV future stalls out in the driveway
Administration drops EV phase-out plan
POTUS REVS the V8 — climate crowd FUMING! 🏎️💨

social.text: you can have my gas pedal when you pry it from my cold dead foot. WE WON.

choices.advisor: A crowd-pleaser at the pump. The transition you're skipping is happening abroad with or without you.

title: Sell the National Park?
briefing: A mining consortium has offered a staggering sum for extraction rights on the edge of a beloved national park. The money could fund the entire parks system for years; the land could never be unspoiled again. Cash the check, or keep the postcard?

choices.label: Lease the buffer lands
choices.result: You open the park's outer rim to extraction and earmark the windfall for trails and rangers. Conservationists call it a bribe to wreck the view.

news.headline: Park self-funds for a decade on smart land deal
President sells the park's edge to the highest bidder
Interior leases park buffer lands for extraction
POTUS PUTS THE PARK ON SALE — your favorite view, now with a mine! ⛏️🏞️

social.text: ranger jobs AND mining jobs in one valley? our school stays open.

choices.advisor: The math is seductive and the scenery is finite. Once that ridgeline goes, no budget buys it back.

choices.label: Reject it and expand the park
choices.result: You not only decline — you annex the disputed land into the park forever. Hikers weep with joy; the consortium and the cash-strapped county do not.

news.headline: Historic: president grows the park instead of selling it
President expands national park, rejects mining bid
Billions in jobs locked away for a pretty view
POTUS SUPERSIZES the park — miners LOCKED OUT forever! 🌲🚫

social.text: walked this ridge for 30 years scared it'd be gone. now it never will be.

choices.advisor: The cleanest legacy move on the board. The county that wanted the money will make you feel it.

choices.label: Order an environmental study
choices.result: You commission a years-long impact review and decide nothing today. Both camps accuse you of stalling — which is, precisely, the plan.

news.headline: President orders study on disputed park lands
Decision dodged as red tape buries a deal
Conservationists wary of a quiet path to drilling
POTUS hits SNOOZE on the park fight — see you in 2030! 😴

social.text: 'we're studying it' is the most presidential way to say 'not today.'

choices.advisor: A study buys time and offends everyone a little. Useful when you genuinely don't want to choose yet.

title: The River Runs Dry
briefing: Two states are at each other's throats over a shrinking river that feeds both. The upstream farms want their historic share; the downstream cities are rationing showers. Governors are calling daily, and there isn't enough water for everyone to win.

choices.label: Broker a forced compact
choices.result: You lock both governors in a room and don't let them out without a signed deal. Nobody gets all they wanted, but the lawsuits stop and the taps stay on.

news.headline: President brokers historic water-sharing compact
A fair-ish peace in the western water war
Farmers forced to give up generational water rights
POTUS SPLITS THE RIVER — two states, one very angry handshake! 🤝💧

social.text: the city can shower again. i don't care who's mad, it worked.

choices.advisor: Thankless and durable. A compact you force today is a war you don't fight every summer.

choices.label: Side with the farms
choices.result: You defend senior water rights and the upstream growers keep their flow. The harvest is saved; downstream cities tighten rationing and seethe.

news.headline: President protects the farmers who feed the nation
Cities left to ration while crops drink the river dry
Administration upholds upstream water rights
POTUS PICKS THE FARMS — city faucets run DRY! 🚿❌

social.text: this water built our farm over a century. finally someone respects that.

choices.advisor: Feeds the nation and the base. The thirsty millions downstream are a much bigger electorate.

choices.label: Prioritize the cities
choices.result: You route emergency flows to the parched metros. Faucets recover and unrest cools — while fallow fields and furious growers dominate the farm-state press.

news.headline: President keeps millions from running out of water
Emergency flows redirected to drought-hit cities
Crops left to die so cities can keep their lawns
POTUS SAVES THE CITIES — farmers watch fields TURN TO DUST! 🌾💀

social.text: 20 days of brown-water warnings just ended. you have no idea.

choices.advisor: More people, more stability, more food-price blowback. There's no clean answer when the river loses.

title: The Last Mine Closes
briefing: The country's last major coal mine is days from shutting for good, taking a whole town's livelihood with it. You can't save the coal — but you can decide what these families wake up to. Prop up the past, or pay to build them a future?

choices.label: Fund a green-jobs transition
choices.result: You announce a retraining and clean-energy investment package built around the town. It's hopeful and expensive, and the miners are watching to see if 'transition' is more than a brochure.

news.headline: President turns a dying coal town toward the sun
Federal package aims to retrain coal workers
Washington bets miners want to build wind turbines
POTUS swaps COAL for SOLAR in dying town — will it work?! ☀️⛏️

social.text: skeptical but i signed up for the training. first hope this valley's had in years.

choices.advisor: The compassionate, future-facing play. If the new jobs don't materialize fast, the cynicism comes back doubled.

choices.label: Subsidize the mine to stay open
choices.result: You write checks to keep the lights on underground a few more years. The town cheers; economists and climate groups call it money set on fire.

news.headline: President stands by coal country's workers
Taxpayers prop up a mine the market already buried
Administration subsidizes last operating coal mine
POTUS REVIVES the mine — coal's back, baby?! (for now) ⛏️💵

social.text: my dad mined here, his dad too. you kept our way of life alive.

choices.advisor: Buys loyalty and time, not a future. You're paying to delay the exact same conversation in three years.

choices.label: Let it close, offer relocation aid
choices.result: You let the market run its course and offer checks to help families move on. It's honest and unsentimental, and the town feels written off.

news.headline: Mine closes as president offers relocation aid
Coal's last chapter ends, but the town is left behind
President abandons coal families to 'the market'
POTUS to coal town: HERE'S A CHECK, GOOD LUCK! 📦😔

social.text: they don't want us to move. they want us to disappear. message received.

choices.advisor: Economically clean, humanly brutal. 'I gave you a check and a map' is not a speech that ages well.

title: Pledge at the Climate Summit
briefing: Every major nation is at the global climate summit, cameras rolling, waiting to hear your number. A bold pledge makes you the world's climate leader but binds your economy to hard targets; a cautious one keeps your hands free and your rivals smirking. The podium is yours.

choices.label: Make the most ambitious pledge
choices.result: You commit to the deepest cuts in the room and rally smaller nations behind you. The world stands and claps; back home, industry reads the fine print and groans.

news.headline: President leads the world with a landmark climate pledge
President announces ambitious emissions commitment
Grand promises abroad, painful bills back home
POTUS WOWS the summit — signs away the smokestacks on LIVE TV! 🌍🎤

social.text: watched the whole hall stand up. our president just led the planet. chills.

choices.advisor: A standing ovation in front of the world. Now you owe the world a delivery you'll be judged on for years.

choices.label: Offer a credible modest pledge
choices.result: You pledge cuts you can actually hit and dare others to match. Diplomats nod approvingly; activists wanted fireworks and got a spreadsheet.

news.headline: President offers measured, achievable climate target
Sensible: no reckless promises at the summit
Cautious pledge falls short of the moment
POTUS plays it SAFE on the world stage — activists yawn 😴

social.text: a promise you can keep beats a headline you can't. underrated move.

choices.advisor: Quietly the smart play — a target you hit beats a target you announce. Just don't expect a parade.

choices.label: Refuse to bind your economy
choices.result: You decline to sign anything that ties your hands and put growth first. Energy markets and sovereignty hawks cheer; allies leave the summit muttering about American retreat.

news.headline: President refuses to mortgage the economy abroad
President walks away as the world commits
President declines binding climate pledge at summit
POTUS GHOSTS the climate summit — allies SHOOK! 🌍🚪

social.text: finally a president who won't let foreigners set our energy policy. respect.

choices.advisor: Protects the economy and your independence. It also hands your rivals the moral high ground on a platter.

title: Revive Nuclear Power?
briefing: Engineers say a new generation of reactors could deliver clean, round-the-clock power and slash emissions for good. But 'nuclear' still spooks the public, the waste question lingers, and the price tag is enormous. Bet big on the atom, or steer clear of the word entirely?

choices.label: Launch a nuclear buildout
choices.result: You greenlight a fleet of next-gen reactors and the clean-firm-power crowd is ecstatic. NIMBY hearings ignite in every proposed county and the protest signs come out of the attic.

news.headline: President bets the climate on a nuclear revival
Finally: clean power that works at 3 a.m.
Bold on carbon, but the waste question won't go away
POTUS GOES NUCLEAR — glow-in-the-dark jokes EVERYWHERE! ☢️⚡

social.text: clean baseload that doesn't care if the wind blows. this is the unlock. FINALLY.
a reactor 8 miles from my kids' school? absolutely not. see you at the hearing.

choices.advisor: Clean, firm, and politically scary. The physics is on your side; the public memory is not.

choices.label: Fund a small pilot program
choices.detail: One demo reactor, prove it first.
choices.result: You bankroll a single advanced demo and promise to scale only if it works. Cautious and credible — boosters wanted a fleet, skeptics wanted nothing.

news.headline: President funds advanced nuclear demonstration plant
Smart first step toward clean, reliable power
A toe in the water while the climate clock ticks
POTUS builds ONE tiny reactor — baby steps to the atom! ⚛️🍼

social.text: prove it on one site before you bet the grid on it. this is how adults do nuclear.

choices.advisor: Low-risk, low-reward. A pilot buys data and cover, and quietly delays the real decision to a future you.

choices.label: Rule out new nuclear
choices.result: You take the atom off the table and double down on wind, solar, and storage. Anti-nuclear voters relax; grid engineers warn you just removed your only clean backstop for the dark, windless nights.

news.headline: President closes the door on nuclear risk
President benches the one clean source that runs nonstop
Administration rules out new nuclear plants
POTUS says NO to nukes — grid engineers SWEAT the night shift! 🌙😬

social.text: no new reactors, no waste my grandkids inherit. thank you for listening.

choices.advisor: Popular and intuitive. It also bets the clean grid entirely on the weather cooperating — every single night.
